semanticCore: Blind from birth, blind state, obscure, or dark
quranicRelevance: Jesus healing the blind from birth (Akmah - 'al-akmaha wal-abrasa').

keyDerivations.meaning: Blind from birth, congenital blindness, or one born without sight

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَأُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَهَ وَالْأَبْرَصَ وَأُحْيِي الْمَوْتَىٰ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ (3:49): And I heal the blind from birth and the leper, and give life to the dead by permission of God (Jesus' biological miracles).
discardedNoise: Obscurity of paths in dark nights (Kamah).
summaryEn: The root represents congenital blindness (Kamah), detailing Jesus' miracle of restoring sight to those born completely blind (Akmah) by divine decree.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر العمى الخلقي الملازم للمولود من بطن أمه (كمه)، مخلداً إعجاز عيسى الطبي بشفاء المولود أعمى (الأكمه) بإذن الله.
rawText: أَرْضٌ كَمْهَآءٌ i. q. مُغِمَّةٌ and مِغَمَّةٌ. (TA in art. غم.)
